secondary_archetype: AI Product Manager with enterprise/solutions delivery flavor

level_signals: Compensation range $178,500–$241,500 consistent with Staff IC at a well-funded AI startup
Expected to define vertical-level roadmaps and influence commercial models

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in professional services (legal, consulting, banking, or adjacent fields) with experience operating in complex, high-stakes workflows.
3+ years of product management experience or closely related roles (e.g., solutions, forward deployed engineering, product consulting).
Experience working in B2B or enterprise environments, ideally with AI, automation, or workflow tooling.
Demonstrated ability to ship and iterate on enterprise-grade software or high-complexity solutions.
Executive presence and a customer obsession mindset
Strong strategic thinking paired with detail orientation—able to zoom out to vertical-level needs while handling precise workflow nuances.
Ability to communicate both qualitatively and quantitatively; strong command of structuring ambiguous problem spaces.
Comfort navigating extreme ambiguity, defining structure from zero, and driving clarity for cross-functional teams.
A scrappy, ownership mentality—willing to jump on flights, run QA, troubleshoot with engineering, or support customer teams when needed.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience building or deploying AI agents, workflow automation tools, or retrieval-augmented systems.
Experience as a forward deployed engineer, solutions architect, or consultant with strong product instincts.
Prior role in a hyper-growth startup or high-performing enterprise product org.

scope.surface: AI agent-powered solutions for strategic enterprise customers across new verticals (legal and professional services)
scope.business_model: B2B enterprise SaaS with professional services / agentic AI
primary_metrics_inferred: Measurable productivity and quality outcomes for customers
Repeatability of bespoke deployments into scalable products
core_responsibilities: Partner directly with strategic accounts to uncover high-impact workflows and translate ambiguous requirements into clear, actionable product definitions for agent-powered solutions.
Lead discovery for your assigned accounts and verticals, developing a deep understanding of user intentions, data constraints, workflows, and success criteria.
Map workflows to the broader product, identifying where out-of-the-box agent capabilities fit and where custom development is needed.
Drive deployments end to end—working with forward deployed engineering and customer teams to implement, test, and refine agent solutions.
Define and track success metrics for the verticals you support, ensuring solutions deliver measurable productivity and quality outcomes.
Develop account-level and vertical-level roadmaps, translating customer needs into scalable products or reusable frameworks.
Collaborate cross-functionally with founders, executives, engineering, and legal research to align product direction and ensure technical feasibility.
Influence new commercial models, including pricing, packaging, and deployment strategies for agent-led solutions.
Champion repeatability, helping transform one-off solutions into product features, components, or templates that scale across the customer base.

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership scope (new verticals, agent-powered solutions)
High-growth company with 1000+ customers in 60+ countries and strong PMF signals
Broad definition of PM experience accepted (solutions, FDE, product consulting)
Role sits at intersection of strategy and execution — high ownership and visibility
red_flags: No location or work authorization details stated — remote eligibility and visa sponsorship are entirely unclear
Role title in identity fields is 'Staff Product Manager, New Verticals' but JD body refers to 'Product Manager, Agents' — possible discrepancy in scope
Equity not mentioned despite being a common component at AI startups at this seniority level
'Willing to jump on flights' implies significant travel expectation not formally quantified
